g.text: What is the policy on international business travel?
Retrieved Evidence · BM25 + query coverage + phrase bonus · intent anchors: international
International business travel requires approval from both the employee’s line manager and department head,
and the request must be submitted at least 14 calendar days before departure with the business purpose,
destination, dates, and estimated cost [KB-003]. Before departure travelers must complete a travel‑risk
briefing, confirm emergency contact details, and obtain corporate travel insurance; travel to a high‑risk
destination additionally requires clearance from Corporate Security [KB-004].
What approval is required before booking domestic business travel?
Retrieved Evidence · BM25 + query coverage + phrase bonus · intent anchors: domestic
Before booking domestic business travel, you must obtain written approval from your line manager before
tickets or accommodation are booked [KB-002]. Employees must also use approved booking channels and select

through the expense system within 10 business

exchange rate for the transaction date; ...
Submit travel expense claims through the expense system within 10 business days after returning. [KB-006]
Required documents: itemized receipts; the approved travel request; and a short business-purpose
For foreign-currency expenses, record the original currency and amount; the expense system will apply the
company’s approved exchange rate for the transaction date, and card statements may be attached as supporting
Can employees work remotely, and how must customer information be handled?
Retrieved Evidence · BM25 + query coverage + phrase bonus · intent anchors: work, information
Employees may work remotely up to two
it. Customer information must not be
Employees may work remotely up to two days per week with line-manager approval when their role and
operational responsibilities permit it [KB-008]. Customer information must not be printed at home or
discussed where unauthorized people may hear it [KB-008].
